title: amylocarpus hylophilus var. macrocarpus
intro: Amylocarpus hylophilus var. macrocarpus, also known as the water hyssop, is a plant species that belongs to the family Thelypodiaceae and is native to Mexico and Central America. The medicinal properties of Amylocarpus hylophilus var. macrocarpus include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and ulcers. The herbal preparations of Amylocarpus hylophilus var. macrocarpus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal remedies.
